Tests switch statement behavior with string cases in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple JavaScript switch statement that demonstrates control flow with string cases. It evaluates the expression `"key"` and compares it against the specified cases. If the expression matches `"another key"` or `"another key2"`, the `assert(false)` statement is executed, which typically indicates a failure or unexpected condition in a testing context. The `default` case contains a `break` statement, which means that if none of the cases match, the switch statement will exit without executing any further code. This code provides narrow functionality, primarily for testing or demonstration purposes, to show how switch statements handle case matching and default behavior.
